title: EscalationMessage
description: Role agent → Lead out-of-scope referral. Sent when a role agent determines that a task (or parts of it) falls outside its competency. The agent does NOT bother the user — it silently refers to the Lead, which splits the task and delegates the out-of-scope parts to the appropriate agents.

to.description: Escalations always go to the Lead agent.

task.description: The task object with status set to BLOCKED.

reason.description: Why this task is out of scope. Must be specific: 'Requires modification of backend API endpoints which are outside my readWrite scope', not 'I can't do this'.

outOfScopeParts.description: Specific parts of the task that are out of scope. Each entry should be actionable enough for the Lead to create a sub-task from it. E.g. 'Add a new REST endpoint at /api/v2/users', 'Update the CI pipeline to include the new test suite'.

completedParts.description: Parts of the task the agent was able to complete within its scope before escalating. These are already done and should not be re-delegated.

suggestedRoles.description: The escalating agent's suggestion for which role(s) should handle the out-of-scope parts. The Lead may override this.
